dead.quote: I lost the case, I lost the vote, and now I'm sitting here like a disbarred man watching the real criminals walk free. [The Gut Player]'s 'instincts' put me in the ground — congratulations, your gut just helped the wolves win. I hope you're proud of that verdict.

winners.quote: Ha! Told you my gut's never wrong — I said the Prosecutor felt off and look who got dragged out! Maybe next time you'll listen to the guy with instincts instead of your little courtroom speeches, yeah?

Textbook Bayesian exploitation — the village discarded their only verified likelihood ([The Prosecutor]'s <Robber> claim) for a prior of zero, while [The Chaos Agent]'s unfalsifiable <Seer> claim absorbed all suspicion. The data never lied; the villagers just refused to update.

losers.quote: [The Prosecutor] needed an audience, and I gave them one. I was so busy listening to their elaborate defense that I forgot to ask the quiet ones what they weren't saying.

Good game — I flung a <Seer> claim at the wall as a <Tanner> just to see what stuck, and somehow [The Prosecutor] ate my bait and got lynched for it. Technically I lost, but watching the whole room chase a ghost I made up? That's the only win I was playing for.

I'm so sorry, [The Prosecutor]... I think you were actually telling the truth about being the <Robber>, but I froze up because I was terrified of being wrong. Now [The Gut Player] and [The Chaos Agent] probably won as werewolves, and I can't stop wondering if one honest sentence from me could have changed everything.
